prompt: Write rotateLeft(a, k) that returns a new array equal to a rotated left by k positions. k may exceed the length. Do not use .slice.
cue: Pull each output element from a wrapped source index.

prompt: Write rotateLeft(a, k) that returns a rotated left by k positions, using .slice. k may exceed the length.
cue: Cut at the pivot and put the tail before the head.
Count backward by the full length.
A delete count of zero removes nothing; the extra arguments are inserted.
A negative start counts back from the end and runs to the finish.
Start after the first, stop one before the end.
Keep the positions whose index is odd.

prompt: Write middle(a) returning the center element of a, which always has odd length.
cue: Halve the length to land on the center index.
mapsTo: LC 876 · Middle of the Linked List (the center index on an array here)

concept: for...in yields index strings, not values
cue: for...in walks an object's keys; an array's keys are its indices, and keys are strings.
for...of hands you each element itself, not its index.

concept: in-place dedup with a write index
prompt: Write removeDuplicates(a) that removes duplicates from the ascending-sorted array a in place, so the first m slots hold the distinct values in order, and returns m (the count of distinct values). Do not build a new array; do not use a Set.
cue: Keep a write index for the next distinct slot; compare each element to the last value you kept.
mapsTo: LC 26 · Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array

concept: in-place remove-value with a write index
prompt: Write removeElement(a, val) that removes every occurrence of val from a in place, packing the kept elements into the front, and returns the number of kept elements. Do not use .filter or .splice.
cue: Keep a write index; copy an element forward only when it is not the value being removed.
